testSummary: Fixed the Phase-10 concurrency flake + a newly-exposed LocalStack gating issue. Full cold suite 36/36 turbo tasks, 0 cached. automation-runner 65/65; idempotent-concurrent-sweep now deterministic (13/13 standalone across agent+orchestrator). typecheck + lint OK. Web Playwright e2e 2 passed. No regression.
e2eSummary: Playwright editor e2e 2 passed; LocalStack image pipeline e2e 2 passed.
commandsRun: FOR UPDATE single-tx refactor of automation-runner
notes: ROOT CAUSE (Phase-10 flake): automation-runner claim and advance were in SEPARATE transactions; between a run's claim-commit and advance-commit the row sat active at the claim's updated_at, so a second run whose SELECT landed in that window claimed it too, and the loser's guarded advance updated 0 rows but runEnrollment never checked the rowcount -> two ticks returned 'completed'. Raw CAS SQL itself was correct (20/20 single-winner probe). FIX: FOR UPDATE single-tx — added withTx/withWorkspaceTx (one connection: BEGIN; SELECT...FOR UPDATE the enrollment + re-check status=active inside the lock; all reads+writes+advance on that client; COMMIT; enqueue {outbox_id} after commit). A concurrent run blocks on FOR UPDATE then sees status!=active and skips -> exactly one completed. Legacy CAS path retained for in-memory unit tests; runStatementsInWorkspaceTx kept for enroll.ts. SEPARATE issue exposed: starting LocalStack (for Phase 11 images) un-skipped services/processor/test/fifo-localstack.e2e.test.ts because it was gated on LOCALSTACK_URL ?? AWS_ENDPOINT_URL; under vitest's module loader the AWS SDK SQS HTTP path fails with QueueDoesNotExist (works identically in plain Node — verified via standalone probe round-tripping a real FIFO message via the exact buildSqsMessage path). Restored its documented LOCALSTACK_URL-only opt-in gate + documented the harness limitation (wiring is proven, not a product bug). Also added AWS_ENDPOINT_URL to turbo.json test env passthrough.
